subtitle: How Stone, Silence and Water Fed the City of Victory

panels.shot: epic wide of boulder-strewn Hampi landscape with a vast tank shimmering beneath the hills
panels.action: The dry, rocky plains around Kamalapura stretch to the horizon, broken by a great man-made tank holding still water under a pale sky, stone embankments hugging its edge.

dialogue.text: In Vijayanagara’s stony heartland, Kamalapura Tank and the Bukka Aqueduct gathered water like treasure, feeding fields, palaces and the sacred City of Victory.

i18n.hi: विजयनगर की पत्थरीली धरती में कमलापुर टैंक और बुक्का नहर ने खज़ाने सा पानी जोड़ा, खेतों, महलों और पावन विजय नगरी को सींचते हुए।

panels.shot: high-angle wide showing parched ground, scattered monoliths, and distant Tungabhadra river ribbon
panels.action: The Vijayanagara Water Engineer stands on a rise above Hampi’s boulders, gazing toward the faint line of the Tungabhadra River that winds far from the dry royal centre.

dialogue.text: This was no lush river valley. Around Vijayanagara, rain was fickle and the Tungabhadra flowed far from the palaces and crowded suburbs.

i18n.hi: ये कोई हरा-भरा नदी घाटी नहीं थी। विजयनगर के चारों ओर बारिश धोखेबाज़, और तुंगभद्रा महलों और भीड़भाड़ वाली बस्तियों से दूर बहती थी।

panels.shot: slow push-in mid-shot on engineer kneeling, drawing lines in dust with a stick
panels.action: The Vijayanagara Water Engineer crouches in the dust, sketching channels and tanks around boulder clusters, eyes narrowed in calculation as farmers watch from a distance.

dialogue.text: To hold an empire here, water had to be guided, stored and shared. Engineers and kings turned stone and slope into a vast hidden machine.

i18n.hi: साम्राज्य बचाना था तो पानी को संभालना, चलाना, बाँटना था। इंजीनियरों और राजाओं ने पत्थर और ढलान को विशाल गुप्त मशीन बना दिया।

panels.shot: epic bird’s-eye over Kamalapura Tank under construction
panels.action: From above, lines of labourers pack earth and stone to build the broad embankment of Kamalapura Tank, while the Vijayanagara Water Engineer stands on the bund, pointing toward the fields beyond.

dialogue.text: South of the royal centre, a great tank rose at Kamalapura. Its embankments and sluices were planned to catch rain and canal water, then release it into waiting fields.

i18n.hi: राजकीय केन्द्र के दक्षिण में कमलापुर में बड़ा टैंक उठा। उसकी मेड़ें और निकास ऐसे रचे गए कि बरसात और नहर का पानी पकड़कर इंतज़ार करते खेतों में छोड़ा जाए।

panels.shot: tight shot along stone-faced bund with sluice mouth
panels.action: The engineer runs his hand along a neatly dressed stone sluice mouth built into the tank bund, the dark opening facing toward low-lying agricultural land.

dialogue.speaker: Vijayanagara Water Engineer
dialogue.text: Through this mouth, we will feed every furrow, season after season.

i18n.hi: यही मुँह हर मेड़ तक पानी पहुँचाएगा, मौसम दर मौसम।

panels.shot: medium wide of inscription being carved on stone slab beside the tank
panels.action: Stonecutters chisel letters into a slab set near the Kamalapura Tank, while the engineer and a local official stand nearby, watching the inscription take form.

dialogue.text: An inscription at Kamalapura recorded the tank. It spoke of a perennial water body that nourished the suburb and fields, tying royal ambition to local soil.

i18n.hi: कमलापुर की एक शिला-लेख ने टैंक का ज़िक्र किया। उसमें सदानीरा जलाशय की बात थी जो उपनगर और खेतों को पालता, राज-इच्छा को स्थानीय मिट्टी से जोड़ता था।

panels.shot: epic wide of watered fields below full tank at dawn
panels.action: At dawn, the full Kamalapura Tank shines like copper, a controlled stream spilling from its sluice to a network of small channels that weave through green paddy.

dialogue.text: From the tank’s full body, water slipped into channels, turning brittle earth into sheets of paddy and sugarcane. The tank became a quiet granary of liquid grain.

i18n.hi: भरे टैंक से पानी नालों में फिसलता, सूखी मिट्टी को धान और गन्ने की चादरों में बदल देता। टैंक चुपचाप तरल अनाज का कोठार बन गया।

panels.shot: wide shot from tank toward distant royal centre on the horizon
panels.action: The engineer stands on the tank bund, looking toward the hazy outline of the royal centre’s gopurams and palaces, stone conduits snaking away from the tank toward the city.

dialogue.text: Kamalapura did more than feed farmers. Canals and channels carried its water toward the royal centre, sustaining kitchens, gardens and ritual spaces of the court.

i18n.hi: कमलापुर ने सिर्फ़ किसानों को नहीं पाला। नहरें और नाले उसका पानी राजकीय केन्द्र तक ले गए, रसोई, बाग़ और दरबार के पूजा-स्थलों को ज़िंदा रखते हुए।

panels.shot: medium shot inside royal precinct, engineer examining terracotta pipes set in trench
panels.action: Within the royal precinct, the Vijayanagara Water Engineer inspects terracotta pipes laid in a trench between stone structures, workers carefully aligning the hollow cylinders.

dialogue.text: Excavations centuries later revealed terracotta pipes around royal buildings. Their neat joints show how water was carried underfoot, kept flowing yet unseen.

i18n.hi: सदियों बाद खुदाई में महलों के पास मिट्टी के पाइप मिले। उनके साफ़ जोड़ बताते हैं कि पानी पैर के नीचे से गुजरता था, चलता हुआ पर नज़र से ओझल।

panels.shot: low-angle view along rock-cut channel hugging boulders
panels.action: The engineer walks beside a rock-cut channel carved into the natural bedrock, the shallow groove curving around boulders toward the city’s core.

dialogue.text: Stone itself was carved into channels. Along slopes and between hills, narrow cuts guided water, using gravity and gradient instead of brute force.

i18n.hi: पत्थर खुद चैनलों में तराशा गया। ढलानों पर और पहाड़ियों के बीच पतली कटिंग ने पानी को राह दी, बल के बजाय गुरुत्व और ढलान का सहारा लेते हुए।

panels.shot: epic wide of Bukka Aqueduct bridging ravine near Anegundi
panels.action: Near Anegundi, the Vijayanagara Water Engineer stands beneath the imposing stone arches and raised channel of the Bukka Aqueduct as it strides across a shallow ravine.

dialogue.text: To move river water across broken ground, they raised it on stone. The Bukka Aqueduct near Anegundi stands today, a long channel on pillars, carrying flow over uneven land.

i18n.hi: टूटी-फूटी ज़मीन पर नदी का पानी चढ़ाने को उन्होंने उसे पत्थर पर उठाया। आज भी आनेगुंडी के पास बुक्का एक्वेडक्ट खड़ा है — खंभों पर उठा लंबा चैनल जो बहाव को ऊबड़-खाबड़ ज़मीन के ऊपर ले जाता है।

panels.shot: close-up of engineer’s hand feeling inner surface of aqueduct channel
panels.action: The engineer leans over the aqueduct, his fingers tracing the smooth inner stones of the water channel as a trickle runs along them.

dialogue.speaker: Vijayanagara Water Engineer
dialogue.text: If the river will not come to the city, the city must climb to the river.

i18n.hi: जब नदी शहर तक नहीं आएगी, तो शहर ही नदी तक चढ़ेगा।

panels.shot: medium shot of stepped tank inside ceremonial area
panels.action: Inside the ceremonial quarter, the engineer stands at the edge of a stepped tank, its geometric stone steps descending toward clear, still water.

dialogue.text: Water was not only for crops and kitchens. Stepped tanks held it for ritual, their descending tiers turning storage into sacred geometry and reflecting towers and sky.

i18n.hi: पानी सिर्फ़ खेतों और रसोई के लिए नहीं था। सीढ़ीदार टैंकों ने उसे पूजा के लिए थामा, उतरती परतों ने भंडार को पावन ज्यामिति में बदला, टावर और आसमान को पानी में उकेरा।

panels.shot: slow push-in on royal centre courtyard near Hazara Rama temple
panels.action: Near the Hazara Rama area, the engineer studies freshly cut stone drainage channels and a newly built straight canal running through a ceremonial court.

dialogue.text: Recent work near Hazara Rama revealed stone drains and a long canal within the ceremonial core, proving water and ritual were woven together in the city’s very heart.

i18n.hi: हज़ारा राम के पास हाल की खुदाई में रस्मी क्षेत्र के भीतर पत्थर की नालियाँ और लंबी नहर मिलीं, साबित हुआ कि पानी और पूजा शहर के दिल में ही एक-दूसरे में बुने थे।

panels.shot: medium shot of engineer overseeing sluice adjustments during dry season
panels.action: In a dry season, the engineer surveys the tank’s water level and instructs workers at the sluice, careful hands turning stone shutters to narrow the outflow.

dialogue.text: Control was everything. Sluices and shutters were adjusted with care so that water lasted through lean months, balancing the thirst of city and countryside.

i18n.hi: काबू ही सब कुछ था। फाटकों और पट्टों को सोच-समझकर चलाया गया, ताकि दुबले महीनों तक पानी टिके, शहर और गाँव की प्यास का संतुलन बना रहे।

panels.shot: wide shot of bustling bazaar and palace kitchens drawing from channels
panels.action: A lively bazaar and palace kitchens bustle beside a small canal, women and servants filling pots while cooks tend large cauldrons in shaded courtyards.

dialogue.text: From tanks and aqueducts, the flow reached markets and halls. Food, trade and ceremony rested on an unseen lattice of channels, pipes and drains.

i18n.hi: टैंकों और एक्वेडक्ट से निकला बहाव बाज़ारों और दीवानखानों तक पहुँचा। अनाज, व्यापार और रस्में एक अदृश्य जाल पर टिकी थीं — चैनलों, पाइपों और नालियों के।

panels.shot: melancholic wide of ruined Vijayanagara with surviving tank and aqueduct
panels.action: Centuries later, the Vijayanagara Water Engineer is seen as a ghostly figure among ruined mandapas, while the Kamalapura Tank and Bukka Aqueduct still stand, holding water in a quiet landscape.

dialogue.text: The empire fell, its palaces broken by war and time. Yet tanks, channels and aqueducts endure, their forms still catching rain and river for today’s villages.

i18n.hi: साम्राज्य गिर गया, महल युद्ध और समय से टूट गए। पर टैंक, नाले और एक्वेडक्ट अब भी हैं, आज की बस्तियों के लिए बारिश और नदी को पकड़ते हुए।

panels.shot: close-up of engineer’s weathered face looking at ripples in water
panels.action: The engineer gazes at small ripples moving across the tank’s surface as a farmer’s child plays along the edge in the present day.

dialogue.text: Archaeologists now trace terracotta pipes, stone drains and canals like veins. They read old inscriptions and new trenches to map the lifeblood of the City of Victory.

i18n.hi: पुरातत्वविद अब मिट्टी के पाइप, पत्थर की नालियाँ और नहरों को नसों सा पढ़ते हैं। वे पुराने शिला-लेख और नई खाइयाँ पढ़कर विजय नगरी की जीवन-धारा का नक्शा बनाते हैं।

panels.shot: epic wide of Kamalapura Tank at sunset, fields and ruins framed together
panels.action: At sunset, the Kamalapura Tank glows gold, flanked by green fields on one side and the silhouettes of Vijayanagara’s ruins on the other, with the engineer standing between.

dialogue.text: The tanks of Kamalapura and the Bukka Aqueduct remind us: empires rise not only on swords and stone, but on patient care for water flowing quietly through their land.

i18n.hi: कमलापुर के टैंक और बुक्का एक्वेडक्ट याद दिलाते हैं: साम्राज्य सिर्फ़ तलवार और पत्थर पर नहीं, बल्कि बहते पानी की धीरजभरी देखभाल पर खड़े होते हैं।

title_i18n.en: The Tanks That Fed an Empire

subtitle_i18n.en: How Stone, Silence and Water Fed the City of Victory
subtitle_i18n.hi: पत्थर, सन्नाटा और पानी से बनी विजय नगरी
